metadata.title: Which Indian Prime Minister Are You Most Like?
metadata.scoring_model: Each answer gives points directly to one or more PMs, embedded in each option.

questions.text: The country is going through a hard time. What do you do first?

options.text: Speak to the people and try to give them hope.
Take quick action and show strong control.
Talk to different people and build agreement first.
Use the moment to inspire people with a bigger goal.

questions.text: What makes a leader's speech powerful?

options.text: A strong idea for the future.
Clear steps and firm direction.
Careful thinking and balance.
Words that make people feel proud and united.

questions.text: A big change is needed, but many people may not like it at first. What do you do?

options.text: Go ahead if it will help the country in the long run.
Bring it in slowly so people can adjust.
First get support from others before acting.
Explain it as part of a bigger plan for the country.

questions.text: Which criticism would upset you the most?

options.text: “You had no big ideas.”
“You were weak when things got difficult.”
“You did not listen to others.”
“You moved too slowly.”

questions.text: What kind of legacy would you want most?

options.text: Building strong national institutions.
Growing the economy and creating new chances.
Making people feel proud of the nation.
Keeping the country steady in difficult times.

questions.text: When choosing your team, what matters most?

options.text: Skill and knowledge.
Loyalty and discipline.
Bringing in different groups and viewpoints.
Energy and bold thinking.

questions.text: How should India deal with powerful other countries?

options.text: Stay independent and think long term.
Make practical deals that help India.
Show strength so India is respected.
Build partnerships that help national growth.

questions.text: How should a leader deal with people who disagree?

options.text: Let them speak, even if it is difficult.
Listen, but do not let them stop progress.
Accept helpful criticism, but stop harmful disorder.
Answer them openly and win the argument.

questions.text: Which line sounds most like you?

options.text: I like building systems that last.
I like getting things done fast.
I like connecting with large numbers of people.
I like thinking deeply before acting.

questions.text: The economy is weak. What is your first focus?

options.text: Long-term reforms.
Help for ordinary people right away.
Big development projects people can see.
Better planning and smoother government work.

questions.text: How do you make tough decisions?

options.text: I think deeply and ask others for advice.
I decide quickly and trust my judgment.
I look at facts and expert views.
I think about what the public will support.

questions.text: What holds a country together best?

options.text: Strong democratic institutions.
Shared culture and identity.
Equal chance to grow and succeed.
Working together for a common goal.

questions.text: People are demanding fast social change. What do you do?

options.text: Support change, but through proper systems.
Lead the change boldly.
Move carefully so the country does not split.
Keep order and accept only what is practical.

questions.text: Which quality matters most in leadership?

options.text: Vision
Discipline
Practical thinking
Charisma

questions.text: What would your critics most likely say?

options.text: You dream too much.
You control too much.
You are too careful.
You think too much about politics.

questions.text: In a national crisis, what do you rely on most?

options.text: My beliefs and values.
Firm control and order.
Public trust.
Support from many groups.

questions.text: Which national goal excites you most?

options.text: Building a modern and strong state.
Helping ordinary people live better.
Making the nation more confident and united.
Changing old systems to help growth.

questions.text: What is the main job of a prime minister?

options.text: Show the country where to go.
Run the government well.
Represent the hopes of the people.
Keep different forces in balance.

questions.text: A government suddenly loses its main leader. What matters most in the next few days?

options.text: Keeping the system calm and stable.
Reassuring people with honesty and dignity.
Making sure rules are followed properly.
Preventing panic while keeping dialogue open.

questions.text: Which problem would you feel most strongly called to fix?

options.text: Unfair treatment of farmers and rural families.
The neglect of villages and basic livelihoods.
A system that feels unfair to ordinary communities.
A political culture that lacks courage and candor.

questions.text: Which kind of respect matters most to you?

options.text: Being trusted in a difficult transition.
Being seen as honest, modest, and dependable.
Being known as someone who stands up for rural India.
Being valued for staying close to people on the ground.

questions.text: Which leadership flaw bothers you most?

options.text: Waste, excess, and lack of discipline.
Ignoring unfairness because it is politically convenient.
Turning disagreement into conflict instead of diplomacy.
Saying safe things instead of hard truths.

questions.text: What kind of strength do you admire most?

options.text: Quiet service without show.
Courage rooted in the lives of ordinary people.
Steadiness when history suddenly changes course.
Discipline that does not bend under pressure.

questions.text: What kind of politics feels healthiest for India?

options.text: Politics that begins with the farmer and the village.
Politics that keeps fairness on the national agenda.
Politics that uses dialogue before confrontation.
Politics that is independent, bold, and unafraid.

results.subtitle: The Nation Builder
results.summary1: You are thoughtful, future-focused, and drawn to big ideas. You naturally think about what a country, community, or institution can become over time — not just what it needs today.
results.summary2: You likely value learning, reason, dignity, and long-term progress. People with your style often stand out for their vision, idealism, and ability to build something that lasts.
results.gentleNote: You may sometimes think so far ahead that others want quicker action, but your strength is seeing the bigger picture.

quote.text: At the stroke of the midnight hour, when the world sleeps, India will awake to life and freedom.

results.subtitle: The Duty Keeper
results.summary1: You are dependable, sincere, and guided by responsibility. You may not seek attention, but people trust you when steadiness is needed.
results.summary2: You likely believe leadership is about service, calm judgment, and doing what is right in important moments. Your strength is not loud power — it is quiet reliability.
results.gentleNote: Your modest style may be easy to miss at first, but people remember how safe and steady they feel around you.

quote.text: The nation is in the grip of a crisis. It is in essence a crisis of character.

results.subtitle: The Servant Leader
results.summary1: You are humble, honest, and deeply grounded. You do not need display or drama to lead well. Your strength comes from sincerity, discipline, and care for ordinary people.
results.summary2: People with your style are often respected because they feel real. You likely value duty, fairness, and simple but meaningful leadership.
results.gentleNote: Your calm style may be overlooked in louder settings, but your leadership feels genuine and deeply trusted.

quote.translation: Hail the soldier, hail the farmer.

results.subtitle: The Strong Decider
results.summary1: You are bold, determined, and powerful under pressure. When things become difficult, your instinct is to step forward and act.
results.summary2: You likely carry a strong sense of authority and responsibility. People with your style are often admired for courage, toughness, and the ability to lead in high-stakes situations.
results.gentleNote: Your forceful style can feel intense to softer personalities, but your gift is that people rarely doubt your resolve.

quote.text: I would like the India of the future to keep this quality while ridding itself of poverty.

results.subtitle: The Principled Administrator
results.summary1: You are disciplined, serious, and guided by standards. You believe leadership should be honest, orderly, and rooted in self-control.
results.summary2: You likely respect rules, structure, and personal integrity. People with your style often stand out for consistency, uprightness, and a strong moral center.
results.gentleNote: At times you may come across as strict, but your strength is that others know exactly where you stand.

quote.text: For a democracy to be healthy and effective...

results.subtitle: The Grounded People's Leader
results.summary1: You are practical, rooted, and close to everyday life. You care about how policies affect real people, especially those outside elite spaces.
results.summary2: You likely value fairness, dignity, and the concerns of ordinary families. People with your style are often respected for being grounded and connected to lived reality.
results.gentleNote: You may prefer the real and immediate over the grand and symbolic, but that is what keeps your leadership human and relatable.

quote.text: Economic development of the country turns on improvement in agricultural practices...
quote.sourceTitle: Ideas of Charan Singh / archival scholarship

results.subtitle: The Modernizer
results.summary1: You are forward-looking, energetic, and drawn to new possibilities. You are naturally interested in change, innovation, and the future.
results.summary2: You likely enjoy fresh ideas and believe systems can be improved. People with your style often appear youthful in mindset, hopeful, and eager to move things forward.
results.gentleNote: Your excitement for change may move faster than some people are comfortable with, but your gift is bringing fresh possibility into the room.

quote.text: For nation-building, the first requisite is peace — peace with our neighbours and peace in the world.

results.subtitle: The Reform-Driven Challenger
results.summary1: You are principled, bold, and willing to question the system when needed. You care about fairness and are not afraid to stand for change.
results.summary2: You likely believe leadership should respond to public concerns and moral pressure, not just protect the status quo. People with your style often seem courageous, reform-minded, and driven by justice.
results.gentleNote: Your desire to correct what is wrong can create friction, but it also gives your leadership moral force.

quote.text: For one, equity is now on the national agenda. No party can ignore it.

results.subtitle: The Independent Fighter
results.summary1: You are bold, self-directed, and not easily controlled by others. You value conviction and the freedom to stand on your own judgment.
results.summary2: You likely have an inner toughness and a strong independent streak. People with your style often come across as fearless, candid, and personally courageous.
results.gentleNote: Your self-directed style can feel intense at times, but it also means people see you as authentic and hard to bend.

quote.text: My government will do everything to take the country forward in all spheres.
quote.sourceTitle: The Long March: Profile of Prime Minister Chandra Shekhar

results.subtitle: The Quiet Reformer
results.summary1: You are strategic, thoughtful, and quietly effective. You do not need noise to make impact. You prefer careful thinking, good timing, and deep judgment.
results.summary2: You likely notice patterns others miss. People with your style often have great inner depth and the ability to guide major change without demanding attention.
results.gentleNote: Because you are not flashy, some may not see your strengths right away, but your leadership often proves itself over time.

quote.text: My objective is to make India truly self-reliant.

results.subtitle: The Consensus Leader
results.summary1: You are balanced, dignified, and able to bring people together. You believe leadership should combine conviction with grace.
results.summary2: You likely value respect, emotional steadiness, and keeping unity alive even when people disagree. People with your style are often admired for maturity, warmth, and trustworthiness.
results.gentleNote: You may move more carefully than impatient people like, but your strength is that others feel heard and steadied by you.

quote.text: We are not to build a new nation but to make this ancient nation virile to face the challenge of modern times.
quote.sourceTitle: Speech reference cited in archival quotation

results.subtitle: The Grassroots Consensus Builder
results.summary1: You are rooted, patient, and attentive to different voices. You believe leadership should stay connected to people on the ground.
results.summary2: You likely value listening, coalition-building, and practical understanding. People with your style are often seen as approachable, grounded, and quietly inclusive.
results.gentleNote: Your low-key style may not chase attention, but it helps others feel that leadership is still close to real life.

quote.text: One should live amidst farmers, till land, and tend cows and buffaloes.

results.subtitle: The Gentle Diplomat
results.summary1: You are thoughtful, courteous, and deeply respectful in the way you lead. You believe relationships, dialogue, and balance matter.
results.summary2: You likely prefer calm intelligence over raw force. People with your style are often admired for tact, decency, and the ability to lower tension rather than raise it.
results.gentleNote: Your soft-spoken style may seem understated to some, but your strength lies in wisdom, tact, and emotional intelligence.

quote.text: India today stands on the threshold of greatness and that greatness is within our reach, within our grasp. We can do it and we must.

results.subtitle: The Thoughtful Technocrat
results.summary1: You are intelligent, composed, and guided by substance over show. You care about sound judgment, careful policy, and doing serious work well.
results.summary2: You likely prefer depth to drama. People with your style are often respected for their calm mind, credibility, and ability to inspire trust through competence.
results.gentleNote: You may not seek the spotlight, but that often makes your leadership feel steadier and more trustworthy.

quote.text: We cannot undo the past, but we have an option, today, to build a better future.
quote.sourceTitle: Prime Minister's statement in Lok Sabha, 12 August 2005

results.subtitle: The People's Connector
results.summary1: You are confident, energetic, and highly connected to public feeling. You understand the power of message, purpose, and national pride.
results.summary2: You likely know how to create momentum and make people feel part of something larger. People with your style are often seen as charismatic, ambitious, and able to inspire strong belief.
results.gentleNote: Your strong public style may feel intense to quieter personalities, but your gift is your ability to move people with conviction.

quote.text: Nation building never happens with immediate thinking; it happens with a big vision, patience, and decisions taken on time.
